reason: DE-15. The <search_context> block was removed per the Phase C extractor skeleton. The hybrid-default search fact already lives on the production path in the search_document bindings.yaml description/summary, which the model sees as the tool description. The base prompt no longer restates the search backend mechanics.
synonyms: The default blended search combines meaning-based and exact-word matching

phrase: Pass `search_mode="lexical"` for exact disease names, ontology IDs, abbreviations, strain names, PMIDs/DOIs, and other controlled tokens.
reason: DE-16. The lexical-mode guidance for exact tokens is carried by the search_document bindings summary, which tells the model exact-word matching is best for specific symbols/strains/alleles/probes/identifiers and that very short queries automatically lean on exact-word matching. The base prompt no longer restates it.
synonyms: Exact-word matching is best for specific gene symbols, strains, alleles, probes, or identifiers

phrase: retrieve ALL chunks under a named section using the LLM-resolved hierarchy, useful for complete coverage of Results, Discussion, figure legends, or Methods context.
reason: DE-18. The full-section coverage fact (read all chunks under a named section, grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order, for full coverage) lives in the read_section and read_subsection bindings summaries on the production path. The base prompt no longer restates the read-tool mechanics.
synonyms: grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order

phrase: The loaded paper was ingested through a PDF processing pipeline into structured chunks annotated with page, section, subsection, and element type.
reason: DE-14. Backend ingestion implementation detail, not a curation rule. The page/section/subsection provenance fields the model actually needs already surface in the read_chunk and record_evidence tool results (and in the shared evidence field shape retained in <output_and_handoff_contract>). No home for the ingestion-pipeline narration; recorded here so the deletion is visible in review.

phrase: Pass `search_mode="hybrid_lexical_first"` when normal hybrid search should retry with lexical-heavy matching.
reason: DE-17. Internal retry-mode mechanic, not a curation rule and not a curator-facing search behavior. The curator-facing search guidance (blended default, exact-word matching for specific tokens, short queries leaning lexical) already lives in the search_document bindings summary. The hybrid_lexical_first retry knob is an implementation detail dropped with no home.

phrase: belong in metadata.exclusions[] (do not stage them)
reason: DE-13/DE-34/template rule 1. INACCURATE for the builder pattern: DiseaseStageInput exposes no exclusion/reason_code parameter, DiseaseDiscardInput takes only a free-text reason, and materialize_disease_builder_state hard-codes metadata.exclusions: [], metadata.ambiguities: [], metadata.notes: [], and run_summary.excluded_count/ambiguous_count: 0 (builder_conversion.py L916-934). The model has NO channel to write metadata.exclusions[]. The curation INTENT is preserved by the accurate rule kept in <success_criteria>/<evidence_and_curation_rules>: an exclusion is expressed by NOT staging the candidate (or discard_disease_observation with a free-text reason). No home for the literal 'belong in metadata.exclusions[]' framing because the channel does not exist; recorded here so the deletion is visible in review.
